text: A partner bank defaulted on a loan.
We reported the breach to the regulator.
The report showed a small loss.
A partner bank defaulted on a short-term loan.
We assessed the exposure and set aside a provision.
We reported the breach to the regulator within two days.
The regulator reviewed our response and did not downgrade the desk.

Hello, this is the supervision office at the Central Bank. We received your incident report about last quarter, and we reviewed it carefully. Thank you for reporting the breach so quickly. Overall, your team handled the situation well, and we did not change your rating. However, we noticed that your early warning system reacted too slowly, so we expect a clear action plan by the end of next month. Please send the updated figures and your new alert model when they are ready. Thank you.

Hi Sergio, it is Daniel from the risk team. I finished reading the first quarter report before our meeting. A few quick points. Volatility spiked in January, and we recorded a small loss in February after the partner bank defaulted. The good news is that March was calmer, and the figures came back to normal. I added a provision for the loss, and I updated all the numbers. Can we talk on Monday about the new alert model? I think we should present the findings to the committee next week. Thanks.

Last quarter began with high volatility. In January, the market spiked and several positions lost value. In February, a partner bank defaulted and the desk recorded a loss. The team assessed the exposure and set aside a provision. Finally, they reported the breach to the regulator, and the regulator did not downgrade the desk.
A partner bank defaulted on a loan.
We reported the breach to the regulator.
The quarter was difficult for the desk.
The regulator did not downgrade the desk.
In March, the numbers came back to normal.
